content.problemStatement:
You are given two **binary** strings AAA and BBB, each of length NNN. Your task is to make your scorescorescore odd which is initially 000 by choosing NNN elements such that:


For each index iii (1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N), you will select either AiA_iAi​ or BiB_iBi​.
If the character you select is equal to "1", add 111 to the scorescorescore. If the character is "0", add nothing.
Your goal is to determine whether it is possible to make the scorescorescore an odd number. If it is possible, print `YES`, otherwise print `NO`.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT, the number of test cases.
For each test case:

The first line contains an integer NNN, the length of the strings AAA and BBB.
The second line contains NNN characters representing the binary string AAA.
The third line contains NNN  characters representing the binary string BBB.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line `"YES"` if it is possible to make scorescorescore an **odd** number and `"NO"` otherwise.


You may print each character of the string in either uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `"yEs"`, `"yes"`, `"Yes"` and `"YES"` will all be treated as identical).
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 3×1053 \times 10^53×105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1:

For index 111, you have to add 111 to the scorescorescore as both A1A_{1}A1​ and B1B_{1}B1​ are 111.
For index 222, you add 111 to the scorescorescore if you select A2A_{2}A2​ (since A2A_{2}A2​ is equal to 111). if you select B2B_{2}B2​ add 000 to the scorescorescore (since B2B_{2}B2​ is equal to 000). Thus, you can add either 111 or 000 to the score for index 222.
Similarly, for index 333, you have the option to add either 111 or 000.
One possible solution is to select A1A_{1}A1​, A2A_{2}A2​, B3B_{3}B3​ which would result in scorescorescore 333.
